calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2020 Continued To Apr-01-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John Kithas for Ann Williams.

The Court inquired about a revised proposed order. Mr. Kithas stated that he has a redlined version and was hoping to go line by line with the court. The Court noted that counsel got probate examiner's notes, sent a letter to the probate examiner contesting but saying they will do this, got another set of notes, and that it seems like it is easy stuff being missed. The Court inquired if the order has the description of the real property; Mr. Kithas responded affirmatively. The Court inquired if the order addressed the distribution of residue upon Rosemary's death. Mr. Kithas responded no, and stated that it would be distributed equally between Rosie and Ann. The Court noted that is not consistent with the promissory note. Mr. Kithas inquired whether various changes were sufficient. The Court instructs Mr. Kithas to submit a clean copy of the proposed order including the language he stated earlier by close of business tomorrow. Mr. Kithas stated he will have it by today.

The second amended waiver of account and report of administrator, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Mar-09-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2019 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
John Kithas for and with Ann Williams.

The Court noted that not all the terms were in the proposed order and should have the same language as the petition and suggested putting in all the language from the trust. Mr. Kithas stated he will try that and use present tense and third person where applicable. The Court also inquired as to the billing for S. Yi who was not admitted into state bar yet, $475.00 filing fees on dates on reflected on the register of actions, as well as Westlaw charges as research fees that are not ordinarily permitted. Mr. Kithas stated this seemed like a simple estate, parties agreed to hourly billing then reduced to ordinary fees, that his office bills $75.00 for paralegals and $150.00 for law clerk and that S. Yi was doing more than a paralegal is now admitted to the bar. The Court noted that it is okay to bill $150.00 for law clerk, to eliminate the filing fee and Westlaw charge. The Second Amended Waiver of Account and Report of Administrator, etc., is continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-25-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Kithas for and with Ann Williams (Petitioner).

Mr. Kithas stated that he received no notice from anyone contesting the will. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order). Bond is waived.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
